task_completion.description: Log into the OpenMRS system using the provided credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenMRS login screen with 'admin' entered, or the OpenMRS home dashboard indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Visit Types management section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenMRS Administration page or the 'Manage Visit Types' screen being displayed in the browser.

task_completion.description: Open the form to add a new Visit Type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Add Visit Type' form or page being actively displayed.

task_completion.description: Enter the required Visit Type name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact text 'Telemedicine' entered into the 'Name' input field on the form.

task_completion.description: Enter the required Visit Type description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact text 'Remote consultation via video or phone' entered into the 'Description' text area on the form.

task_completion.description: Save the new Visit Type and verify it is added to the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a success notification (e.g., 'Visit Type saved') or the 'Telemedicine' entry visible in the list of active (unretired) Visit Types.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with OpenMRS rather than terminal commands or API scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a standard web browser interface (like Chrome or Firefox) navigating the OpenMRS web application.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database queries (e.g., MySQL via terminal) to insert the record.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal windows open showing SQL INSERT commands or database prompts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine OpenMRS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard OpenMRS UI elements, headers, and styling, ensuring the agent didn't just type the expected text into a generic text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected Visit Type name is exactly 'Telemedicine'. The expected description is exactly 'Remote consultation via video or phone'. The created visit type must be active (not retired).
